skill.name: mermaid
skill.description: Guide for creating GitHub-compatible Mermaid diagrams with readable styling for light and dark modes.
skill.summary: Create Mermaid diagrams that render clearly in GitHub Markdown across light and dark modes.
skill.author: BfdCampos

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, malware keywords, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and one hardcoded URL. Manual review found a documentation-only SKILL.md file: the command findings are Markdown code fences or inline code, and the keyword findings are diagram text or color values. The only real risk factor is a user-facing mermaid.live preview link, with no automatic network request or data exfiltration evidence.

low_findings.title: Static command-execution detections are Markdown examples
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many backtick locations as Ruby or shell execution. Manual review shows these are Markdown inline code spans and Mermaid fenced examples, not executable code paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown guidance and contains no scripts, shell invocations, or runtime code. The flagged syntax appears inside documentation examples or inline formatting.

low_findings.title: Malware keyword and cryptography detections are false positives
low_findings.description: The C2, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance detections map to normal words, Mermaid labels, frontmatter, and hexadecimal color values. No evidence found of command-and-control behavior, cryptographic operations, system probing, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review confirms the flagged tokens are diagram documentation, color literals, or ordinary prose. There is no executable context where these tokens could perform malicious actions.

low_findings.title: External preview link requires user discretion
low_findings.description: The skill links to mermaid.live as an optional place to preview diagrams. This is a user-initiated external website reference, not an automatic network request by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is clearly present, but it appears only in documentation. The risk is limited to users choosing to paste diagram content into an external preview service.

content.value_statement: Mermaid diagrams can become unreadable or fail to render in GitHub Markdown. This skill gives practical styling and syntax guidance for diagrams that work in light and dark modes.
actual_capabilities: Provides a GitHub-compatible Mermaid flowchart template.
Recommends dark fill and light stroke color pairings for readability.
Explains subgraph label syntax that avoids GitHub parse errors.
Shows node shape patterns for users, actions, decisions, and databases.
Documents common Mermaid mistakes in GitHub Markdown.
Suggests preview testing with an external Mermaid renderer.
limitations: Does not render, validate, or execute Mermaid diagrams by itself.
Focuses mainly on flowcharts rather than every Mermaid diagram type.
Uses opinionated styling rules that may not match every brand system.
External preview testing may expose diagram content to a third-party site.

use_cases.title: Document pull request architecture
use_cases.description: Create a readable Mermaid flowchart that explains components, requests, and outputs in a GitHub pull request.

use_cases.title: Standardize system diagrams
use_cases.description: Apply consistent node shapes, subgraph labels, and color styles across repository documentation.

use_cases.title: Improve Markdown documentation visuals
use_cases.description: Replace fragile or hard-to-read diagrams with GitHub-friendly Mermaid examples for process and data flow docs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple flowchart
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub-compatible Mermaid flowchart for this process. Use dark fills, light strokes, rounded nodes, and quoted subgraph labels: [describe process].

prompt_templates.title: Revise an existing diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Mermaid diagram for GitHub Markdown. Fix syntax issues, improve light and dark mode readability, and explain the main changes: [paste diagram].

prompt_templates.title: Design an architecture diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mermaid architecture diagram for these components and interactions. Group related services with transparent subgraphs and use consistent class styles: [list components].

prompt_templates.title: Build a documentation standard
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a reusable Mermaid diagram style guide for our repository. Include node roles, color pairs, subgraph rules, and examples for architecture, process, and data flow diagrams.

output_examples.input: A request flows from a user to an API, then to validation, processing, and a success response.
output_examples.output: A GitHub-ready Mermaid flowchart with a user circle, rounded process nodes, a transparent processing subgraph, and success styling.

output_examples.input: A Mermaid diagram has light fills, unquoted subgraph labels, and Font Awesome icon text.
output_examples.output: A corrected diagram plan that uses dark node fills, quoted labels, rounded shapes, and plain text labels instead of unsupported icons.

output_examples.input: A data pipeline includes ingestion, validation, enrichment, storage, and reporting stages.
output_examples.output: A structured flowchart layout with grouped pipeline stages, readable color classes, and clear arrows between each stage.

best_practices: Use dark node fills with light strokes and white text for light and dark mode readability.
Keep Mermaid class definitions near the top so styling is easy to maintain.
Quote subgraph labels with spaces and keep subgraph fills transparent.
anti_patterns: Do not use Font Awesome icons in GitHub Mermaid diagrams because they render as text.
Do not use light node fills with dark text when diagrams must work in dark mode.
Do not leave subgraph labels with spaces unquoted because GitHub may fail to parse them.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create Mermaid diagrams that render clearly in GitHub Markdown and remain readable in light and dark modes.

faq.question: Does the skill run Mermaid or generate image files?
faq.answer: No. It provides writing and styling guidance. Rendering and validation require another Mermaid tool or preview service.

faq.question: Why does it recommend dark fills and light strokes?
faq.answer: That pattern keeps nodes readable against both light and dark GitHub page backgrounds.

faq.question: Can I use Font Awesome icons in these diagrams?
faq.answer: The skill advises against them because GitHub Mermaid rendering does not support those icons reliably.

faq.question: Is the external preview link required?
faq.answer: No. The mermaid.live link is optional. Users should avoid pasting sensitive diagrams into external services.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
